Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

On calendar:
DEFENDANT CHERYL WALLACE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Specially Appearaing DEFENDANT CHERYL WALLACE's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied.

A motion to quash can challenge personal jurisdiction over a defendant. However, "[d]omicile in the state is alone sufficient to bring a[] . . . defendant within the reach of the state's jurisdiction for purposes of a personal judgment?." (Allen v. Superior Court (1953) 41 Cal.2d 306, 310-311.) Defendant does not challenge jurisdiction on the basis that she is not domiciled in California, and her own proof of service states that she is "domiciled in California." (Def.'s Mot, pg. 5.)

A motion to quash can also challenge lack of jurisdiction where there has not been valid service of summons. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 CA4th 403, 413.) However, Defendant's motion does not appear to challenge jurisdiction on the basis of improper service, and the proof of service states that she was served through personal service on October 30, 2020. (Plf.'s Opp., Ex. 2.) The summons is in proper form, and there is no genuine ambiguity as to the court in which the lawsuit is pending. By order filed January 7, 2020, the court granted plaintiff's ex parte application to correct defendant's name in the caption of the lawsuit.

Defendant's remaining arguments are not properly raised in a motion to quash. Defendant shall file an answer or other responsive pleading within 10 days of the court's order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Proceeding not reported.
